title: sql fail if use scalar subquery with alias
poc: create table test1 ( id int, name varchar(20));
create table test2 ( id int, name varchar(20));

select
 (select count(id) as cnt1 from test1 ) as c1,
 (select count(b.id) as cnt2 from test2 b where a.id = b.id) as c2
 from  test1 a;

select
 (select count(id) as cnt1 from test1 ),
 (select count(b.id) as cnt2 from test2 b where a.id = b.id)
 from  test1 a;

select
 (select count(id) as cnt1 from test1 ) ,
 (select count(b.id) as cnt2 from test2 b where a.id = b.id) as c2
 from  test1 a;

select
 (select count(b.id) as cnt2 from test2 b where a.id = b.id) as c2,
 (select count(id) as cnt1 from test1 ) as c1
 from  test1 a;
key_bug_features: scalar subquery with alias causes SQL failure
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test3 ( id int PRIMARY KEY, value float, description varchar(50));
INSERT INTO test3 (id, value, description) VALUES (1, 10.5, 'First entry');
INSERT INTO test3 (id, value, description) VALUES (2, NULL, 'Second entry');
SELECT (SELECT COUNT(id) AS total FROM test3) AS total_count, (SELECT AVG(value) FROM test3 WHERE value IS NOT NULL) AS average_value FROM test3;
SELECT (SELECT MAX(value) FROM test3) AS max_value, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test3 WHERE description LIKE 'First%') AS first_count FROM test3;
